mnemonics.title: SPM Key Activities
mnemonics.content: Use phrase: 'PReSCoR' - Planning, Risk, Scheduling, Cost, Resources, Control/Closure

mnemonics.title: Risk Management Process
mnemonics.content: Use phrase: 'IRAM' - Identification, Response, Assessment, Monitoring

mnemonics.title: Project Life Cycle Phases
mnemonics.content: Use phrase: 'IPECC' - Initiation, Planning, Execution, Control, Closure

mnemonics.title: Cost Estimation Techniques
mnemonics.content: Use phrase: 'EAPB' - Expert judgment, Analogy, Parametric, Bottom-up

acronyms.usage: Hierarchical decomposition of project scope into smaller components
Network planning using probabilistic time estimates
Technique to identify longest path determining minimum project duration
Integrated scope, schedule, cost performance measurement
Stakeholder responsibility matrix for role assignment
Standard guide for project management practices

visualTips.visualization: Draw project network as a flow diagram; highlight the longest path in red - this path determines minimum duration
Think of a horizontal calendar where each bar represents a task - bar length = duration, position = start/end time
Imagine an inverted tree or organization chart - top is final deliverable, branches split into smaller work packages
A 3x3 grid with Probability (low/medium/high) on X-axis and Impact on Y-axis - plot risks in appropriate quadrants

analogies.analogy: Like a ship captain, the PM charts the course (planning), monitors weather (risks), directs the crew (resources), and steers to destination (goals)
Like architectural blueprints break a building into floors, rooms, and components, WBS breaks project into phases, deliverables, and work packages
Like a narrow neck in a bottle limits flow, critical path activities constrain overall project completion - focus effort here first
Like insurance protects against unforeseen events, risk management prepares contingency plans to minimize negative impacts
